critical_invariants: Work queue entry format: exactly 32 bytes per entry - must not change (function_ptr(8), arg(8), priority(4), flags(4), result(8))
Lock-free submission: CAS (LOCK CMPXCHG) on tail pointer must remain atomic
Queue memory at 0x120000 (8KB) must not change base address
Priority ordering: higher priority values must be dispatched before lower ones
Dispatch must verify entry is valid (non-null function pointer) before execution
Submit must return error (carry flag set) when queue is full, never silently drop work
Status query must be safe to call from any CPU without locking
known_opportunities: Priority queue optimization: replace linear scan with binary heap for O(log n) dispatch
Work-stealing improvement: idle CPUs steal from busy CPU queues using lock-free dequeue
Per-CPU work queues to eliminate contention on single shared queue
Batch submission: accept array of work items with single atomic tail update
Cache-line aligned queue entries (pad 32-byte entries to 64 bytes) to prevent false sharing
Adaptive spinning: spin briefly then MONITOR/MWAIT when queue is empty
NUMA-aware scheduling: prefer dispatching to CPU on same NUMA node as data
Speculative dispatch: begin executing probable next work item while current completes

immutable_regions.reason: Work queue entry structure offsets are hardcoded in submit/dispatch - changing layout breaks ABI
